message:
fix(installer): make TOML config writer idempotent and handle duplicate entries

The Codex config.toml writer had three bugs that caused TOML parse errors
on subsequent installs:

1. Duplicate section headers: if both [mcp_servers.mcp-agent-mail] (hyphenated)
   and [mcp_servers.mcp_agent_mail] (underscore) existed, both were kept —
   TOML treats these as different keys, causing Codex to fail on load.

2. Sub-table collision: if http_headers was defined both inline
   (http_headers = { Authorization = "..." }) and as a sub-table
   ([mcp_servers.mcp_agent_mail.http_headers]), the awk script kept both
   forms, producing invalid TOML.

3. Bare hyphenated keys: the regex only matched the quoted form
   [mcp_servers."mcp-agent-mail"] but Codex uses the bare form
   [mcp_servers.mcp-agent-mail].

Fixes:
- Normalize hyphenated section headers (bare or quoted) to underscore form
- Detect and skip duplicate section headers (keep only the first)
- Absorb sub-table headers (e.g. .http_headers) into inline syntax
- Verified idempotent: running the installer twice produces identical output

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
